text:
KINGS NEUROSCIENCES CENTRE


Clinic Date: 25 March 2022

Dr M Mistry
Ridgeway Health Centre

12 Ridgeway Lane, Southwark, London SE5 8JL
Dear Dr M Mistry
Alex Martin, DOB: 14-02-1990, Hospital No: H739256 NHS No. 8472035691
Flat 3, 21 Brook Terrace, London SE15 3QA 

Diagnoses/summary: Recurrent unprovoked seizures of uncertain classification. No definitive structural lesion identified on prior routine MRI (2018) per patient report. Previous routine EEG reportedly non-diagnostic (2019). No history of prolonged status events. Pre-conception counselling visit.

Current issues: Planning pregnancy within the next 12–18 months. Wants to review medication safety, folate, driving and lifestyle guidance. His mother accompanied him and helps with seizure documentation.

Seizure history and recent course: He continues to experience convulsive events without a clear aura or consistent lateralising features. There have been intermittent brief collapses with limb stiffening and post-event confusion. His mother notes an overall improvement compared with previous years, with nine tonic seizures documented in 2022 so far. No recent injuries. Triggers are uncertain; sleep loss and missed meals are suspected contributors. No alcohol excess. No illicit drugs. No history of cardiac syncope.

Medication history:
- Current: Levetiracetam 1 g twice daily; Folic acid 5 mg once daily (initiated three months ago in anticipation of conception); Vitamin D3 1000 IU daily.
- Previous: Sodium valproate (stopped 2020 due to patient preference and concern regarding teratogenicity in future pregnancy planning), carbamazepine (discontinued 2017 due to dizziness), lamotrigine (trial in 2019—rash after dose escalation, stopped), clobazam PRN (rare use—sedation).
- Not tried: Topiramate, zonisamide, lacosamide, perampanel, brivaracetam.

Allergies: No known drug allergies. Past rash with lamotrigine re-challenge.

Comorbidities: Mild asthma; seasonal allergic rhinitis. No known cardiac disease. BMI 24. No history of nephrolithiasis.

Family/social: Lives with partner; both engaged in family planning discussions. Works part-time in IT support. Does not drive currently; aware of DVLA regulations and self-reported last event one month ago.

Examination today: Alert, oriented, normal language and cranial nerves. Motor exam full power, normal tone. No cerebellar signs. Gait normal. Blood pressure 118/72.

Investigations: We will request baseline serum levels (U&E, LFTs, FBC, vitamin D) and a trough levetiracetam level for reference, acknowledging that therapeutic ranges are clinical. I will also arrange an updated EEG including sleep recording. MRI brain (epilepsy protocol) to be considered if EEG or clinical course suggests a focal correlate.

Pre-conception counselling discussion (patient-reported preferences noted):
- Medication: We discussed maintaining the fewest effective medicines at the lowest effective doses during the periconception period. He prefers to remain on levetiracetam if control is acceptable. We reviewed that dose adjustments may be needed in pregnancy due to pharmacokinetic changes; therapeutic drug monitoring was explained.
- Folic acid: Continuing 5 mg daily pre-conception and through the first trimester; he has already commenced this. We discussed adherence and timing.
- Safety and lifestyle: Importance of regular sleep, stress management, and consistent meals. Seizure first-aid reinforced. Avoid swimming unaccompanied and heights. Shower rather than bath.
- Driving: We reiterated DVLA guidance and that he should not drive given recent seizures; he understands and agrees to update DVLA as required.
- Perinatal care: We outlined multidisciplinary antenatal care with obstetrics and neurology input. Partner was invited to attend future appointments.

Plan:
- Continue levetiracetam 1 g twice daily; continue folic acid 5 mg once daily.
- Arrange nurse-led telephone follow-up in 6–8 weeks to review adherence, side effects, and seizure diary, with a view to dose optimisation if events persist.
- Order blood tests (U&E, LFTs, FBC, vitamin D) and a reference levetiracetam level; book updated routine+sleep EEG.
- Provide written pre-conception information leaflet and signpost to Epilepsy Action resources.
- Consider alternative or add-on therapy only if seizure burden increases or adverse effects emerge; options previously untried were outlined today for future discussion.
- Routine clinic review in 4 months, sooner if seizures cluster, injuries occur, or if pregnancy is confirmed.

Thank you for your continued care. Please let me know if any issues arise or if earlier review would be helpful.

Yours sincerely

entities.text: nine tonic seizures documented in 2022 so far
His mother notes an overall improvement compared with previous years, with nine tonic seizures documented in 2022 so far

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter dated 25 March 2022 states: "nine tonic seizures documented in 2022 so far." We must convert this into a rate. As of 25 March 2022, "so far" plausibly covers January through March (about 3 months). Therefore, 9 seizures over approximately 3 months → 9 per 3 months. No other explicit frequency is provided that is higher. The patient is not seizure-free and no 6-month seizure-free period is indicated.
seizure_frequency_number: nine tonic seizures documented in 2022 so far
reference: His mother notes an overall improvement compared with previous years, with nine tonic seizures documented in 2022 so far
